caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied mix is four-digit 5414 and carries graphic-design/art-direction contamination from sibling code 541430.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 37.39% residual is a bounded judgment rather than a reweighted six-digit occupational structure.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: S3 measures business-tool use among Houzz design respondents, not observed task-level automation of 541410 wage roles.
inputs.pi_dist: S4 provides practitioner examples rather than a representative target-size revenue split.
inputs.pi_dist: This models pass-through only; it does not claim that product procurement or human coordination is an AI labor saving.
inputs.pi_moat: Sources establish the service bundle, not a measured firm-level retention or pricing-moat rate.
inputs.pi_moat: Licensing varies by state; this estimate does not use regulation as a blanket moat.
inputs.pi_moat: Principal portability is a separate deal-level risk that may lower the value for residential boutiques.
inputs.t50_years: S3 measures any AI tool use among Houzz design-firm respondents, not a 541410 target-band measure of effective task adoption.
inputs.t50_years: This timing does not use succession, market size, transaction activity, or the expected terminal-demand class.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The numeric mapping is a judgment from a qualitative ‘nearly one-third’ statement and the survey is not restricted to NAICS 541410.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The target pool may have higher basic tool use, but that does not necessarily imply higher effective use in client-facing design work.
inputs.historical_analogs: Neither source is a longitudinal 541410 productivity study or a direct estimate of future task displacement.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: S6 studies succession planning in 63 architecture and interior firms under 40 employees, not owner ages, NAICS-only firms, or the $1–10M EBITDA band.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This demographic proxy is used only for buyability, not adoption or capture.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: The study is limited to 63 firms under 40 employees and combines architecture with interior design; it does not measure seller willingness or target-quality supply.

inputs.active_consolidators: S7 proves one acquisition in private-club planning/interiors; it does not establish a PE platform, a national consolidator count, or buyer interest in ordinary residential studios.
inputs.buy_mult: S8 is one NYC-metro commercial design-and-build deal with repeat revenue, approved-vendor status, and remodeling exposure; it is not a standalone 541410 comp set.
inputs.buy_mult: S9 is broker marketing guidance, not a disclosed transaction database.
inputs.buy_mult: The frozen dataset’s receipt-based margin estimate includes product pass-through and should not substitute for target-level normalized EBITDA.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a forward underwriting estimate, not an observed forecast or a claimed multiple-expansion thesis.
inputs.exit_mult: Service mix, client concentration, procurement margins, and retention of creative principals can move the actual exit materially.
inputs.pricing_structure: S4 is not a representative target-band revenue survey, and S1 is a product taxonomy rather than a financial dataset.

inputs.pricing_structure: S4 directly shows flat-fee, percentage-based, product-cost and markup examples, but it does not establish the prevalence of hourly or cost-plus/procurement billing in the target population.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $342,107 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541410); class <5: 13,213 firms, avg revenue/firm $590,121 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $92,354 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,938 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,036,537 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $318,718 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 673 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,873,851 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $606,258 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 275 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,288,840 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,610,203 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 25 firms, avg revenue/firm $24,612,240 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,851,816 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,695,462 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,368,840 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 313

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: S1 and S2 establish an output that mixes design production with contract administration, procurement, and installation support. S3 directly shows current AI use in routine design-firm operations. Fractions are task judgments for the locked supplied roles: visual/document production and administrative work are more replaceable than client interpretation, site work, contractor coordination, and physical logistics.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541410 ($4,053,951,989) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541410 ($18,324,956,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied mix is four-digit 5414 and carries graphic-design/art-direction contamination from sibling code 541430.

inputs.current_adoption_pct: The target pool may have higher basic tool use, but that does not necessarily imply higher effective use in client-facing design work.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. The sources support the pressed-but-surviving demand thesis, current AI-use direction, succession gap, one strategic acquisition, and the stated valuation anchors. Extensive authored proxy and scope caveats remain necessary, and S4 supports a narrower set of fee and markup examples than the full pricing mix inferred from it; neither issue reverses a factor or changes the kill thesis.

sources.title: NAPCS Product List for NAICS 54141: Interior Design Services
sources.what_it_supports: Defines interior-design services and separately describes full-service contract administration, design-only services, consulting, decorating, and furnishings resale by establishments classified in 541410.

sources.title: Interior Designers — Occupational Outlook Handbook
sources.what_it_supports: Describes interior-design tasks including CAD/BIM visualizations, plans, material selection, ordering, installation oversight, contractor coordination, site visits, and varied state licensing requirements.

sources.title: 2025 U.S. Houzz State of AI in Construction & Design
sources.what_it_supports: US survey of design-only residential/commercial firms: nearly one-third use AI, 50% of firms with 10+ employees use it, and current applications are concentrated in routine administration/content rather than complex client management.

sources.title: How did you land on your fee and markup strategies?
sources.what_it_supports: Trade-practice examples of flat design fees and fee-plus-markup models, published in collaboration with the 2024 Interior Design Business Survey partners.

sources.title: ASID Releases 2025 Economic Outlook Report
sources.what_it_supports: ASID’s current demand context: an uneven outlook affected by high interest rates, material costs, tariffs, labor constraints, and opportunities in adaptive reuse and senior living.

sources.title: New Research Explores Succession Planning For Interior Design Firms
sources.what_it_supports: ASID/Greenway research on 63 architecture and interior design firms under 40 employees, including low formal succession-plan rates and a leadership gap.

sources.title: Chambers Acquires C2 Limited Design Associates
sources.what_it_supports: A verified 2026 strategic acquisition of a planning and interior-design firm by a firm providing planning, architecture, interior design, and procurement services.

sources.title: Firm Announces Deal Closing For An Interior Design & Build Services Company
sources.what_it_supports: Reports a 2025 NYC-metro commercial interior design-and-build company appraised at 3.2x trailing EBITDA, with repeat revenue, approved-vendor status, and other favorable attributes.

sources.title: Selling an Interior Design Business — Valuation Insights
sources.what_it_supports: Broker-published directional interior-design EBITDA range of 4x–6x and stated importance of firm size, client portfolio, reputation, and service diversity.

title: Interior Design Services
